source: Lage Raho Munna Bhai (2006) — full screenplay; weighted to 'Gandhigiri', the imagined Bapu, the 'Get well soon' campaign and 'chemical locha'
love_type: both — broad blockbuster, AND cult that minted a real word ('Gandhigiri') adopted into public discourse and protest culture
core_emotion: the warmth of discovering that the gentlest possible response can defeat a bully — goodness reframed as the strongest move, not the weakest

dialogue_style.register: Mumbai tapori (street-goon) slang from Munna fused with Gandhian moral vocabulary — high comedy from the collision of registers
dialogue_style.rhythm: gangster bluster colliding with saintly calm; Circuit's loyal-idiot one-liners as percussion
dialogue_style.stylization: heightened comedy grounded by a real emotional ache (Munna's love, a hallucinated mentor)
dialogue_style.why_it's_theirs: only this film would have a gangster earnestly explain Gandhi to other gangsters — the voice IS the premise

signature_dialogues.construction: coinage — Gandhi + the '-giri' suffix of 'dadagiri' (thuggery); rebrands non-violence with the swagger of gangsterism
signature_dialogues.the_setup: Munna, a goon, 'meets' Gandhi and starts solving problems with truth and kindness instead of fists
signature_dialogues.presentation: demonstrated, not defined — we SEE Gandhigiri work (the 'Get well soon' campaign) before the word fully lands, so the term arrives already proven
signature_dialogues.payoff: makes non-violence feel cool and macho rather than meek — a gangster's stamp of approval on Gandhi
signature_dialogues.why_quotable: a ready-made label for 'fighting nicely'; entered real Indian protest/news vocabulary

signature_dialogues.construction: weaponized politeness — flowers and get-well cards sent to a villain who isn't sick, until the kindness itself becomes unbearable pressure
signature_dialogues.the_setup: Lucky Singh has wronged Munna; instead of revenge, Munna mounts a relentless campaign of public good wishes
signature_dialogues.presentation: staged as escalating, almost absurd kindness — the more cards arrive, the more the villain unravels; the gentleness is the assault, and the script lets it build like a siege
signature_dialogues.payoff: the bully is defeated by being out-niced in public — guilt does what violence couldn't
signature_dialogues.why_quotable: became the template for real 'Gandhigiri' protests (sending flowers to corrupt officials)

signature_dialogues.construction: tapori euphemism — a 'chemical disturbance' in the brain explains why Munna sees Gandhi; comedy that protects the film's magic-realism
signature_dialogues.the_setup: a doctor's diagnosis for Munna's hallucinations of Bapu
signature_dialogues.presentation: tossed off as slang, which lets the film keep its sincerity (is Gandhi real?) ambiguous and unpreachy
signature_dialogues.payoff: lets the audience hold the mentor as both real and imagined without the film having to choose
signature_dialogues.why_quotable: everyday shorthand for 'something's off in the head' — affectionate, reusable

the_peaks.moment: the 'Get well soon' Gandhigiri campaign that breaks Lucky Singh without a single blow
the_peaks.why_loved: proves kindness can be a winning weapon — deeply satisfying moral wish-fulfilment

the_peaks.moment: Munna's quiet conversations with the imagined Bapu
the_peaks.why_loved: makes a national icon feel like a personal friend/mentor — intimacy with the sacred

wish_fulfillment: the fantasy that you can beat corrupt, powerful bullies WITHOUT becoming like them — winning while staying good
character_magnetism: Munna: a lovable goon with a gangster's confidence and a child's sincerity; Circuit's absolute loyalty as the friendship everyone wants
audacity: rebooting Mahatma Gandhi as a hallucinated buddy-mentor to a gangster — risking irreverence toward a national saint and earning love instead
portability: 'Gandhigiri' became a real word and a real protest tactic; 'Get well soon' flowers as activism
why_cult_not_just_good: audiences took its method OUT of the cinema and into real-world activism — the film changed behaviour, not just mood
transferable_love_triggers: Coin a single word that rebrands a virtue with the swagger of its opposite (non-violence as '-giri') — a portable label is a love-magnet.
DEMONSTRATE the new idea working before you name it, so the term arrives already proven and the audience trusts it.
Let the hero defeat the villain by out-kinding them in public; moral wish-fulfilment beats violent revenge for warmth and rewatchability.
Use a comic euphemism to protect a magic-realist premise (a hallucinated mentor) so you keep sincerity without forcing the audience to choose what's real.
Give the moral mentor an intimate, casual relationship with the hero so a 'big' idea feels like a personal friendship.
confidence: high — 'Gandhigiri', 'Get well soon', 'chemical locha', 'Bapu' all confirmed verbatim; real-world adoption of 'Gandhigiri' is well documented
